content:
Hi Dear ,
Thank you for your message.
Could you please provide more detailed information about the issue? For example, it would be very helpful if you could share a video or some photos showing the current status of the device and its behavior.
You may also refer to our wiki tutorial for reflashing the device:
https://wiki.seeedstudio.com/reComputer_Industrial_Getting_Started/#flash-jetpack
After we receive the additional details, we will further check the issue and advise you on the warranty/service process.

ticket_subject: 用户Alex咨询有关购买Nvidia Jetson Nano Super Developer Kit 8GB的信息，包括电源电压要求、可能导致开发套件问题的电压以及是否有相关文档。
content:
Re: Ticket #348770 - Jetson Developer Kit Support

Dear Alex,
Thank you for your email and your interest in the Nvidia Jetson Nano Super Developer Kit 8GB. We are glad to answer your questions in detail below:
1. Power Supply Voltage Specification
The standard rated input voltage for the Nvidia Jetson Nano Super Developer Kit 8GB is 5V DC. We strongly recommend powering the kit via the DC barrel jack (5.5mm outer diameter / 2.1mm inner diameter, center-positive) with a 5V power supply that can deliver a continuous current of at least 4A, to ensure stable operation under full load.
2. Voltage Range That Causes Abnormal Operation
The safe input voltage operating range of the developer kit is 4.75V to 5.25V DC. Input voltage below 4.75V (undervoltage) will cause unstable operation, automatic restart, or failure to boot; input voltage above 5.25V (overvoltage) may trigger permanent damage to the on-board components and the core module.
3. Technical Documentation for the Board
Yes, we provide complete technical documentation for this developer kit. You can access the official hardware design guide, user manual, power specification, getting started tutorials and other supporting resources through the NVIDIA Jetson official developer portal, as well as the dedicated product page on Seeed Studio Wiki. 
If you have any other questions about the product, please do not hesitate to contact us.

ticket_subject: Recomputer robotics j401 kernel erro
content:
Problem Analysis
Your Recomputer Robotics J401 device is experiencing kernel-related malfunctions, with three core issues:
Typo in apt-mark hold command: An incorrect package name prefix was used, leaving critical NVIDIA Jetson kernel packages unlocked. Subsequent execution of apt upgrade mistakenly upgraded these packages to the generic 36.4.7 version, which is fully incompatible with the Seeed-customized 36.4.3 image for J401 (with integrated GMSL camera drivers).
initramfs (initrd) boot file generation failure: Mismatched kernel package versions caused errors in generating the bootloader file, triggering dpkg error code 1 and a startup failure of the nv-l4t-usb-device-mode.service.
nv_tegra_release configuration conflict: The system attempts to overwrite the Seeed-customized hardware configuration with the generic NVIDIA configuration file. If replaced, it will completely break compatibility between the GMSL camera and the kernel, rendering the device inoperable.
Solution (Execute commands in terminal one by one)
Step 1: Handle the nv_tegra_release configuration prompt (critical selection)
When the system prompts:
Replace the existing configuration in /etc/nv_tegra_release with the new version?
Please select Keep, DO NOT select Replace
This file stores the core configuration of the Seeed-customized 36.4.3 image for J401 (including GMSL camera drivers and hardware adaptation parameters). Replacement will result in total loss of hardware compatibility and device malfunction.
Step 2: Repair the broken package management system
Run the following commands one by one (sudo password required; execute one command before the next):
Fix incomplete dpkg configuration
sudo dpkg --configure -a
Purpose: Resolve unresolved package configuration conflicts and prepare for subsequent repairs.
List installed nvidia-l4t packages (verify correct names)
dpkg -l | grep nvidia-l4t
You will see packages such as nvidia-l4t-bootloader and nvidia-l4t-kernel; confirm the prefix is l4t (not the incorrect 14t).
Lock all core nvidia-l4t kernel packages (critical step to prevent accidental upgrades)
This command automatically locks all NVIDIA Jetson kernel-related packages to avoid accidental upgrades via apt upgrade:
sudo apt-mark hold $(dpkg -l | grep nvidia-l4t | awk '{print $2}')
Verify the lock status
sudo apt-mark showhold | grep nvidia-l4t
A list of locked nvidia-l4t packages confirms successful locking.
Repair the corrupted initramfs boot file
sudo update-initramfs -u -k all
Purpose: Regenerate the bootloader file compatible with the current kernel and resolve initrd-related errors.
Step 3: Fix the failed USB device mode service
Check service status
systemctl status nv-l4t-usb-device-mode.service
View detailed logs of the service startup failure.
Restart the service
sudo systemctl restart nv-l4t-usb-device-mode.service
Enable the service on boot
sudo systemctl enable nv-l4t-usb-device-mode.service
Step 4: Verify system integrity (confirm successful repair)
Run the following commands to check system normality:
Check L4T version (must be 36.4.3, not 4.7)
cat /etc/nv_tegra_release
The correct output includes:
# R36 (release), REVISION: 4.3, GCID: 38968081, BOARD: generic, EABI: aarch64, DATE: Wed Jan  8 01:49:37 UTC 2025
# KERNEL_VARIANT: oot
# Seeed Image Name mfi_recomputer-robo-orin-nx-16g-j401-gmsl-6.2-36.4.3-2025-08-21.tar.gz
# branch R36.4.3
Check kernel version (must match Seeed-customized 6.2 kernel)
uname -r
Check for broken packages
sudo dpkg --audit
sudo apt check
No output from both commands indicates the package system is fully restored.
Important Warnings
Prohibited commands: NEVER run sudo apt full-upgrade or sudo apt dist-upgrade
These commands bypass package locks and force-upgrade nvidia-l4t kernel packages, causing system damage again.
Safe system update method: Update only non-NVIDIA regular security packages
Run the command below; the system skips locked nvidia-l4t packages and updates only safe, non-core components:
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade --with-new-pkgs
Never replace /etc/nv_tegra_release: This file contains J401-exclusive core hardware configurations. Replacement will disable GMSL camera, kernel adaptation, and hardware drivers entirely.
Last Resort: Re-flash the official image
If the above steps fail to repair the system, please re-flash the official J401 image provided by Seeed.Flash BSP with Jetpack to Selected Jetson | Seeed Studio Wiki;Seeed Jetson Cross-Platform Debugging Guide | Seeed Studio Wiki

ticket_subject: Compatibility Inquiry: ZED X One GS with reComputer Mini J501 GMSL Carrier Board
content:
HI,

At the moment, we have not adapted ZED to our GMSL solution. Supporting a new GMSL configuration is a fairly complex process, so we do not have any guidance documentation available.
If your order volume is significant, we can offer a paid customization service for the new GMSL adaptation. The ZED X One camera itself uses a GMSL2 + Fakra interface, and the reComputer mini J501 uses the MAX96712 deserializer, which is also a four-channel solution. Therefore, in theory, this adaptation should be feasible as a paid custom project.

content:
Hi Thomas Becker,

Understood your situation. You may send back all the carrier board accessories for inspection.

ticket_subject: Reachy-mini wireless wifi problems
content:
Hi,

the Reachy Mini wireless network is unavailable. Troubleshoot in this order for the quickest fix:

First, check if the hotspot is broadcasted: After powering on and waiting a moment, the robot should create a Wi-Fi hotspot named **reachy-mini-ap** with the password **reachy-mini**.

If the hotspot is visible: Connect to it, then open the following address in your browser
http://reachy-mini.local:8000/settings, enter your home Wi-Fi name and password, and click **Connect**.

If connecting to your home Wi-Fi fails: The hotspot will usually reappear, and you can try again.

Perform an update and restart first: The official recommendation is to restart the robot and your computer, then go to the settings page and click **Check for updates**.

If the hotspot does not appear at all: Double-check the switch position on the head board—it must be set to **debug**, not **download**. As noted in the official troubleshooting guide, an incorrect switch position may prevent the Raspberry Pi from starting properly, resulting in no hotspot broadcast.

Useful links:
Settings page: http://reachy-mini.local:8000/settings
Wireless getting started guide: /Robotics/Robot_Kits/ReachyMini/platforms/reachy_mini/get_started
Troubleshooting: /Robotics/Robot_Kits/ReachyMini/troubleshooting
Switch position reference image: https://github.com/pollen-robotics/reachy_mini/raw/develop/docs/assets/wireless_switch.png

ticket_subject: Re:Flashing Issue with Jetson Orin Nano on Seeed reComputer Mini Carrier Board (JetPack 6.2.2)
content:
Hi,
thank you for sharing the logs—they’re very helpful for us.  
From the logs, a fatal error occurred at the MB2 stage: EEPROM read failure. This prevents the system from obtaining the module's hardware configuration information (such as serial number, MAC address, calibration data, etc.), resulting in a boot abort. This is most likely caused by hardware damage.

If the device is still under warranty, please return it to Seeed for inspection.
* Note: When packing your device, include all accessories. 

To proceed, please fill out a ticket at the following link: https://aftersale.seeedstudio.com/

Please check the images and the .pdf instructions on how to create such a ticket. After you've done that, please share us the after-sales ticket number: (A-0000-XXXX)

We appreciate your patience and understanding. Feel free to ask me if you have more questions.

However, if your order was not placed directly with us but through another distributor, we regret that we cannot process the after-sale service using their order number. content:
Hello,

I am currently working with a reComputer Robotics J401 (Jetson Orin Nano) using the GMSL expansion board and trying to bring up a MAX96712 + MAX96717 + AR0522 camera setup.

I have followed the device tree references provided here:

https://github.com/Seeed-Studio/Linux_for_Tegra/tree/r36.4.3/source/hardware/nvidia/t23x/nv-public/nv-platform
tegra234-j401-p3768-0000+p3767-0004-recomputer-robo-gmsl.dts
tegra234-j401-p3768-0000+p3767-recomputer-robo-common.dts

However, the MAX96712 is not responding over I2C.

Below is a summary of the debugging steps already performed.

Device Tree runtime verification

The runtime device tree confirms that the expected topology is loaded:

cam_i2cmux (i2c-mux-gpio)
 ├─ i2c@0 -> max96712_a@2e
 └─ i2c@1 -> max96712_b@4e

The node:

      max96712_b@4e

is present and has:

status = "okay"
compatible = "maxim,max96712"

The kernel creates the client:

i2c-10 / 10-004e

GPIO mapping confirmed
From the runtime device tree and GPIO inspection:

mux-gpios   -> gpiochip1 line 15 (PCC.03)
reset-gpios -> gpiochip0 line 122 (PY.00)

These match the phandles:

mux-gpios   = <0x105 0x13 0x0>
reset-gpios = <0xf3 0x90 0x1>

I2C probing results
The MAX96712 does not respond on any expected combination:

Channel 0
bus 9  / addr 0x2e -> no response
bus 9  / addr 0x4e -> no response
Channel 1
bus 10 / addr 0x2e -> no response
bus 10 / addr 0x4e -> no response

Both i2cget and i2ctransfer return:
Remote I/O error

Reset testing 
The MAX96712 reset GPIO (PY.00) was toggled manually using libgpiod, but the device still does not respond on the I2C bus.

Hardware checks

Physical measurements confirm:

Power rails present
Reset line toggling
I2C SCL activity visible when attempting transactions

However, the MAX96712 still does not ACK on any tested address.

Question

Given the above results, I suspect one of the following:

The device tree topology does not match the exact hardware revision of the GMSL board.
The MAX96712 requires an additional power-enable or initialization sequence before responding on I2C.
The expected I2C address or mux channel differs for this camera configuration.

Could you please confirm:

the correct DTS configuration for J401 + GMSL board + MAX96717 + AR0522
whether additional power-enable GPIOs or regulators must be activated
the expected I2C address and mux channel for the MAX96712 in this setup

Any reference bring-up documentation or working DTS example would be very helpful.

Thank you.

Best regards

ticket_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#evgeni_mi@mer-group.com;]
content:
Hi，
Thank you for the detailed description.
Based on your test result, since swapping cameras reproduces the same behavior on the same physical channels, the issue appears to be related more to the affected PoE ports/channels than to any specific camera.
To help isolate whether this is caused by PoE power delivery or by the Ethernet data link itself, could you please help perform the following checks?
Confirm PoE is enabled on the system
Please first make sure PoE has been enabled on the unit according to the official usage guide.

Test the affected port with external camera power
On the problematic channel, please power the camera using an external power source and use the RJ45 cable only for data.
If the interface comes up normally in this case, it would suggest the issue is related to the PoE power delivery on that channel.
If the interface still remains DOWN, then the problem may be related to the Ethernet link path on that port.
Test each physical LAN port one by one
Please record the behavior for each fixed port (for example LAN1 / LAN2 / LAN3 / LAN4) under these conditions:
one camera connected
two cameras connected
three cameras connected
This will help confirm whether the issue is consistently tied to the same physical ports.
Collect logs during reconnection
When the issue happens, please share the outputs of the following commands:
ip -br link
ethtool <interface_name>
dmesg -T | tail -n 200
journalctl -k -n 200
Please run them at the moment when the third camera is delayed or stuck during PoE reconnection.
Once we receive the above test results, we can further determine whether this is a PoE power channel issue or an Ethernet link issue on the affected port.

ticket_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#supertoasted@gmail.com;]
content:
Hi,
Thank you for the detailed information.
Could you please take a short video showing the exact issue, including the power-on behavior of the board? This would help us better understand the current condition and confirm whether there is any abnormal hardware behavior.
Also, could you please try following our wiki guide below to flash the device first, and then check whether it can boot normally afterward?
https://wiki.seeedstudio.com/recomputer_j501_mini_getting_started/#-flash-jetpack-os
We would like to rule out whether this is a software issue before proceeding further. The Seeed J501-Mini getting-started guide specifically includes the JetPack flashing workflow and notes Ubuntu host requirements for that process. 
Once you have tried flashing the system, please let us know the result.

ticket_subject: 回复：[## 347511 ##] Honeywell Image for reComputer Super J4012
content:
Dear Elster,
Thank you very much for your support and for sharing your positive experience with the reComputer Super J4012 in your AI Vision project.
We are very glad to hear that the device has been running stably in production and provides a solid foundation for your object detection use cases.
Regarding JetPack 7.2, we are also closely following NVIDIA’s official JetPack roadmap. As of now, NVIDIA’s current JetPack 7 release track is publicly listed through JetPack 7.1, and JetPack is the official software stack for the Jetson platform. Once NVIDIA officially releases JetPack 7.2, we will synchronize our firmware update accordingly to support the new version as soon as possible. 
We truly appreciate your interest in scaling up with our Jetson hardware series. We will continue to align our software support with NVIDIA’s official releases and improve our platform for production AI workloads.

ticket_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#daniel@mimirsoftware.com;]
content:
Dear Daniel,
Thank you for your message.
According to the reComputer Industrial reference documentation, the reComputer Industrial J4012 supports a DC power input range of 12V to 24V through a 2-pin terminal block. Based on this, it should be possible to power the device from a 12V vehicle outlet, provided that a suitable and stable DC adapter/cable is used. 
For compatibility, we recommend using a regulated 12V vehicle power adapter that can connect to the 2-pin terminal block input of the device. Please also make sure the output polarity and wiring match the device power input correctly. 
Please note that the included standard power adapter is 19V, but the device itself is designed to accept 12V–24V DC input via the terminal block.

For compatibility, we recommend using a regulated 12V vehicle power adapter that can connect to the 2-pin terminal block input of the device. Please also make sure the output polarity and wiring match the device power input correctly. 
Please note that the included standard power adapter is 19V, but the device itself is designed to accept 12V–24V DC input via the terminal block.

ticket_subject: 回复：[## 347370 ##] J401 GMSL image – missing i2c_atr module preventing MAX SerDes initialization
content:
Hi Natalia,
Thank you for your detailed explanation.
Based on the camera model you are using, it appears that your camera is not included in our current supported camera list for the reComputer Robotics J401.
Please refer to our official wiki and datasheet for the supported GMSL camera models and hardware information:
Wiki: https://wiki.seeedstudio.com/recomputer_jetson_robotics_j401_getting_started/
Datasheet: https://files.seeedstudio.com/products/NVIDIA-Jetson/reComputer_robotics_J401_datasheet.pdf
According to our official documentation, the currently supported GMSL camera models are:
SG3S-ISX031C-GMSL2F
SG2-AR0233C-5200-G2A
SG2-IMX390C-5200-G2A
SG8S-AR0820C-5300-G2A
Orbbec Gemini 335Lg
Since your AR0522-based GMSL2 camera is not on this compatibility list, we cannot guarantee that it will work correctly with our current J401 GMSL image and driver package.
raw:
Hi Natalia,

ticket_subject: Problem with reServer Industrial J4012
content:
Hi Evgeny,
Based on your description—especially the 20–30 second power drop and recovery on the affected port—this behavior is often indicative of insufficient input power causing the system to temporarily shut down or throttle PoE output during peak demand.
Recommended next steps:
✅ Try replacing the current power adapter with a higher-wattage, stable unit (we recommend using an adapter rated for at least 20–30% above your calculated max load).
✅ Ensure the input voltage is within the supported range: our ReServer units support DC input up to 36V. Using a voltage too close to the lower limit under load may also cause instability.
✅ If possible, test with only one high-power camera per unit to isolate whether the issue correlates with total power consumption.
Once you've had a chance to test with an upgraded power supply, please let us know the results. If the issue persists, we can proceed to investigate firmware-level PoE negotiation logs or hardware-specific channel behavior.
We're committed to helping you resolve this quickly. Feel free to share any additional logs or photos of your setup if helpful.

content:
Hi Frason,
Thank you for the detailed information.
Based on your description, it appears that the issue is caused by using the wrong system image for your device.
You mentioned that your unit is a reComputer Robotics J4012, but the image you flashed is:
mfi_recomputer-super-orin-nx-16g-j401-6.2-36.4.3-2025-05-22.tar.gz
This is not the correct image for the reComputer Robotics J4012.
Please download the correct Robotics image from our wiki here:
https://wiki.seeedstudio.com/recomputer_robotics_j401_getting_started/#prepare-the-jetpack-image
After downloading the correct image, please follow the flashing instructions in the same wiki to reflash the system.
Using the correct Robotics image should resolve the first-time setup issue you are seeing.

ticket_subject: 客户购买的Seeed Studio工业盒式电脑其中一台在使用30分钟后重启时出现故障，现要求退换货并咨询解决方案。
content:
Hi,
I’m very sorry for the inconvenience this has caused you.
Before we proceed to the next after-sales service step, could you please try reflashing the system by following our wiki guide below?
Seeed Studio Wiki – Flash JetPack:
https://wiki.seeedstudio.com/reComputer_J4012_Flash_Jetpack/#flash-jetpack
This guide is specifically for flashing JetPack OS to the J401 carrier board / reComputer J4012 series, and it recommends using an Ubuntu host computer with a USB Type-C data cable for the process.
After reflashing, please check whether the device can boot normally.
The reason we suggest trying a reflash first is that, based on your description, the issue is likely related to system configuration or software dependency problems rather than a hardware fault. In addition, if the unit is returned for repair, our technical team would also first attempt to reflash the system as part of the troubleshooting process. Since reflashing is already part of the standard diagnostic workflow in our wiki, asking you to try it first may help resolve the issue more efficiently and save time in the overall support process.
If the device still cannot boot after reflashing, we would recommend proceeding with the next step of after-sales repair or replacement support.

ticket_subject: reComputer J4011,reComputer Industrial J4011,reComputer Robotics J4012 with GMSL extension board,reComputer Super J4012,Jetson Orin NX 8Gb,Jetson Orin NX 16Gb
content:
Dear Jose Pablo Roberti,
Thank you for your update and for providing the detailed logs.
Based on the current logs, we can make a preliminary conclusion that the issue is not a normal Wi-Fi configuration or driver loading problem. Instead, it appears that the RTL8822CE module is not being correctly enumerated by the system.
This is because the RTL8822CE uses PCIe for Wi-Fi and USB for Bluetooth, but in your logs we can see that:
no Realtek Wi-Fi PCIe device is detected,
no corresponding Bluetooth USB device is detected, and
the dmesg log also shows a PCIe message: “Phy link never came up”.
Based on this, we would recommend first checking the following hardware-related points:
whether the RTL8822CE module is installed correctly in the M.2 Key E slot,
whether the module is fully inserted and making proper contact,
whether the module itself is functioning normally.
If possible, we also recommend cross-checking with:
another RTL8822CE module on the same device, or
the current module on another compatible device.
If the other device can detect the module normally, then it is very likely that there is a hardware issue with the current device. In that case, we would suggest proceeding with after-sales service / repair.

How long has your device been deployed? Is it running at full load continuously or intermittently? In addition, have any overloaded devices or expansion modules been connected externally?

ticket_subject: 客户询问如何通过GPIO引脚或网络唤醒功能从SC7模式唤醒reComputer Industrial J4012设备，并提到可能需要参考相关文档以确定具体操作方法。
content:
Dear Alex,
Thank you for your update.
I’m glad to hear that wake-up via the PWR_BTN method and via a connected USB keyboard both work on your side.
Regarding your question, at this moment we do not have a new method to achieve wake-up externally without opening the device, other than the approaches already tested. As previously mentioned, LAN wake-up is not supported on this platform.
For practical use, the most feasible workaround would be to use an external access method for the PWR_BTN signal, so that the wake-up trigger can be provided without opening the enclosure. Another possible workaround is keeping a supported USB input device connected for wake-up.

ticket_subject: 回复：Re: 回复：Re: 回复：Re: 回复：[## 342868 ##] reServer J501 : Technical Assistance - ROOTS
content:
Hi,

Yes, the BMCU (Board Management Control Unit) handles power management-related functions for the Jetson AGX Orin module. The BMCU is the EFM8SB10F8G microcontroller (U61 on Sheet 09)  that manages:

Power sequencing: Controls CARRIER_POWER_ON and MODULE_POWER_ON signals to the Jetson module
Power button debouncing: Handles the power button input and shutdown sequences
Reset control: Manages SYS_RST_IN_N and reset timing
Force shutdown: Provides emergency power-off functionality via EFM8_FORCE_SHUTDOWN
Power good monitoring: Monitors power rail status (PS_1V0_PG, PS_1V8_PG, etc.)

To determine the UART protocol and baud rate:
- Check Silicon Labs Documentation
EFM8SB10F8G Datasheet: Review UART0 module specifications (supports up to 460800 baud)
Refer to AN1095 (UART Communication) and AN1389 (Low Power UART)

To determine the UART protocol and baud rate:
- Check Silicon Labs Documentation

Refer to AN1095 (UART Communication) and AN1389 (Low Power UART)

Please refer to our official wiki and datasheet for the supported GMSL camera models and hardware information:
Wiki: https://wiki.seeedstudio.com/recomputer_jetson_robotics_j401_getting_started/
Datasheet: https://files.seeedstudio.com/products/NVIDIA-Jetson/reComputer_robotics_J401_datasheet.pdf

SG3S-ISX031C-GMSL2F
SG2-AR0233C-5200-G2A
SG2-IMX390C-5200-G2A
SG8S-AR0820C-5300-G2A
Orbbec Gemini 335Lg
Since your AR0522-based GMSL2 camera is not on this compatibility list, we cannot guarantee that it will work correctly with our current J401 GMSL image and driver package.

ticket_subject: Power options for the reComputer Robotics J3011 with GMSL Extension - Intelligent Edge AI Computer with NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano Super 8GB
content:
Hi Derek,
Thank you for your question.
For the reComputer Robotics J401, the GMSL function requires the optional GMSL expansion board. Based on our current validated support list, the compatible GMSL camera models are:
SG3S-ISX031C-GMSL2F
SG2-AR0233C-5200-G2A
SG2-IMX390C-5200-G2A
SG8S-AR0820C-5300-G2A
Orbbec Gemini 335Lg
For your requirement of a non-fisheye camera with approximately 100° horizontal FOV / 90° vertical FOV and around 1920 x 1080 resolution, the best candidates from the currently supported list would be:
SG2-IMX390C-5200-G2A – this is a non-fisheye 2MP GMSL2 camera and supports 1920 x 1080 @ 30 fps
SG2-AR0233C-5200-G2A – also a non-fisheye 2MP GMSL2 camera
SG8S-AR0820C-5300-G2A – a non-fisheye 8MP GMSL2 camera if you would like a higher-resolution option
Please note that the documentation confirms these cameras support different focal length lenses, so a medium FOV configuration may be possible depending on the exact lens option. However, from the currently published materials, I cannot confirm an exact 100°H / 90°V lens variant for all of the above models.
Regarding compatibility, yes — the de-serializer is important. Our reComputer Robotics GMSL Board uses the MAX96712 de-serializer, and it is compatible with GMSL cameras that use MAX96717 or MAX9295A as the serializer. So when evaluating third-party or alternative GMSL cameras, this is the key point to verify.
Also, on J401 you will need to make sure:
The GMSL expansion board is installed
The system image includes the GMSL expansion board driver
The correct Jetson-IO overlay is selected based on the camera type (for example, 3G / 6G / Orbbec)
If you would like, I can also help narrow this down to the most suitable model for your application once you confirm whether your priority is RGB image quality, low-light/HDR performance, or exact lens FOV.
Refer:
      https://wiki.seeedstudio.com/recomputer_jetson_robotics_j401_getting_started/#hardware-connection-8
      https://files.seeedstudio.com/products/NVIDIA-Jetson/reComputer_robotics_J401_datasheet.pdf

Dear Shota Itoyama,
Thank you for your detailed request and for providing complete order, failure, and shipping information.
We have confirmed your information as below:
Order Number: REACHYMINI-2360
Product: Reachy Mini Wireless Kit
Defective part: Motor ID 16 (Stewart 6)

We will process your replacement order within 1–2 business days and provide you with the shipping information once it is available.
If you have any further questions, please feel free to contact us.

ticket_subject: Re: SeeedStudio Jetson AGX Orin H01 – GPIO Pin Usage and Configuration
content:
Subject: Re: Follow-up on SeeedStudio Jetson AGX Orin H01 GPIO Pin Usage and Configuration
Dear Gilbert,

Thank you for your prompt reply and follow-up questions regarding the GPIO configuration of the Jetson AGX Orin 32GB H01 Kit. We greatly appreciate your technical feedback and are glad to provide targeted solutions for your two core requests: the complete 40-pin GPIO mapping reference, and the modification of default GPIO pin power-on states.

1. 40-Pin GPIO Mapping Reference

We fully understand that a clear pin mapping (physical pin → BCM number → kernel line number) is critical for your precise GPIO development and deployment. The 40-pin expansion header layout of the H01 Kit is fully identical to the official NVIDIA AGX Orin Dev Kit, so you can directly refer to NVIDIA’s standard 40-pin GPIO mapping as your core reference.

For daily on-device debugging, you can use NVIDIA’s official command-line tools to check real-time pin configuration and mapping directly, which is simple and efficient for practical development:

# View real-time configuration of all 40-pin header pins
sudo /opt/nvidia/jetson-io/config-by-pin.py

# List all supported expansion headers and valid pin ranges
sudo /opt/nvidia/jetson-io/config-by-pin.py -l

# Check detailed configuration of a specific physical pin (e.g., pin 7)
sudo /opt/nvidia/jetson-io/config-by-pin.py -p 7

2. Modification of Default GPIO Pin "ON" Power-On State

Your observation that partial GPIO pins default to HIGH level at power-on is a common hardware and firmware characteristic of the Jetson Orin platform. This default state is determined by pinmux configuration in the bootloader/UEFI phase (before Linux kernel loads), not by user-level OS or applications, which is why direct post-boot software adjustment cannot permanently change the power-on default state.

Root Cause of Default HIGH State

During system boot, three stages dominate pin initialization: 1) Hardware reset with internal pull-up resistors; 2) Bootloader firmware pre-configuration; 3) Kernel device tree pinmux loading. Some pins are also temporarily occupied by peripheral subsystems (I2C, camera, etc.) during boot, leading to a temporary HIGH level before driver initialization completes.

Recommended Solutions (Sorted by Practicality & Stability)

Solution 1: Permanent Modification via Device Tree & Pinmux (For Mass Production)

This is the only fundamental way to fix the default power-on state, suitable for batch deployment. You will need to modify the pinmux .dtsi configuration file, recompile the device tree, and flash the updated DTB to the device. A sample core configuration for setting default LOW level is as follows:

/* Example: Set target GPIO pin to default LOW at power-on */
GPIOXX {
    nvidia,function = "gpio";
    nvidia,pull = "down";      /* Enable internal pull-down resistor */
    nvidia,tristate = "disable";
    nvidia,enable-input = "enable";
    nvidia,drive = "0";        /* Set default output to LOW (1 for HIGH) */
};

Solution 2: User-Space Control with gpiod Toolchain (For Prototyping)

For rapid prototype development, we highly recommend the gpiod toolchain (Linux kernel native GPIO API) instead of the incompatible Jetson.GPIO library. It avoids mapping conflicts and supports stable real-time pin control post-boot:

# Install gpiod and Python binding
sudo apt update && sudo apt install gpiod python3-gpiod

# Check all GPIO line status and kernel line numbers
gpioinfo

# Force set a pin to LOW/HIGH (replace line number with your actual value)
sudo gpioset gpiochip0 135=0  # Set to LOW
sudo gpioset gpiochip0 135=1  # Set to HIGH

Python Code Example for Stable GPIO Control:

import gpiod

# Initialize GPIO chip and target line
chip = gpiod.Chip('gpiochip0')
target_line = chip.get_line(135)  # Replace with actual kernel line number
# Request output mode and set initial level
target_line.request(consumer="railsentry_app", type=gpiod.LINE_REQ_DIR_OUT)
target_line.set_value(0)  # Force LOW immediately after boot

Check for Driver Occupancy Conflicts

If some pins remain unconfigurable, use this command to check for kernel driver occupancy (e.g., camera, I2C peripherals):

cat /sys/kernel/debug/gpio

You can disable conflicting peripheral drivers in the device tree if they are not required for your project.

Attached: GPIO Technical Resources & Links (Sorted by Usage Scenario)

We have compiled targeted official technical links matching your GPIO configuration needs, including standard pin mapping, pinmux modification and debugging guides for your direct reference:

1. Core Tools & Official Documentation

- NVIDIA Jetson-IO Official Documentation (with Standard GPIO Mapping): https://docs.nvidia.com/jetson/archives/r38.2/DeveloperGuide/HR/ConfiguringTheJetsonExpansionHeaders.html 
Description: Official NVIDIA developer guide covering 40-pin standard mapping, Jetson-IO GUI/CLI usage and gpiod operation examples

- NVIDIA Jetson Device Tree Overlay Guide: https://developer.ridgerun.com/wiki/index.php/NVIDIA_Jetson_-_Device_Tree_Overlay 
Description: Step-by-step guide for DT overlay creation, compilation and deployment via Jetson-IO

2. Developer Forum Deep Dives

- PINMUX & GPIO Change Overlay Discussion: https://forums.developer.nvidia.com/t/pinmux-gpio-change-overlay/350431 
Description: Official forum thread covering pinmux modification and default state adjustment

- JetPack 6.0 Dynamic GPIO Setup Discussion: https://forums.developer.nvidia.com/t/how-to-dynamically-set-gpio-in-jetpack-6-0dp/277715/16 
Description: gpiod Python examples and tristate mode troubleshooting for JetPack 6.x

- Chinese Developer Forum GPIO Configuration: https://forums.developer.nvidia.cn/t/jeton-orin-nano-gpio/26198/1 
Description: Practical guide for replacing Jetson.GPIO with libgpiod

3. GPIO Default State Solutions (Match Your Query)

- GPIO Default Power-On State Configuration: https://nvidia-jetson.piveral.com/jetson-orin-nano/gpio01-configuration-and-default-state-on-nvidia-jetson-orin-nano-dev-board/ 
Description: Targeted solution for default HIGH level issue with pinmux code examples

4. Supplementary Resources

- NVIDIA Jetson Linux Archive Documentation: https://docs.nvidia.com/jetson/archives/ 
Description: Archive for all Jetson Linux versions, including Pinmux and hardware adaptation guides

Please let us know the specific physical pin numbers that default to HIGH and your target power-on state, and we will provide customized pinmux configuration guidance and line number confirmation.

Thank you for your trust and technical cooperation.

Best regards,

Neo Zhang

Seeed Studio Technical Support Team

ticket_subject: CSI Camera integration error with Recomputer super J4012
content:
Dear Customer,
Thank you for contacting Seeed Studio Technical Support. We greatly appreciate your detailed feedback on the CSI camera integration issue you are facing with your reComputer Super J4012 (Jetson Orin NX 16GB).
We have carefully analyzed the error logs and test results you provided. The error
nvbuf_utils: dmabuf_fd -1 mapped entry NOT found
is a known issue on the Jetson platform when using CSI cameras, which is usually caused by Argus multi-process resource competition or NVMM/DMA buffer allocation failure. According to practical experience from the NVIDIA Developer Community, this problem can be fixed with several reliable solutions.
Issue Analysis
Based on your description:
✅ The IMX219 camera is correctly detected by the system (verified by nvargus_nvraw and I2C detection)
✅ The camera connects successfully ("Producer has connected")
❌ The streaming pipeline fails during the NVMM/DMA buffer handover stage
This means the driver and hardware communication are normal, while the problem lies in the resource allocation conflict inside the user-space multimedia framework (Argus/GStreamer).
Recommended Solutions
1. Single Terminal with Startup Delay
If you launch multiple camera streams in the same terminal or script, add a short delay between the two commands to avoid resource racing:
gst-launch-1.0 nvarguscamerasrc sensor_id=0 ! queue ! nv3dsink &
sleep 2 && gst-launch-1.0 nvarguscamerasrc sensor_id=1 ! queue ! nv3dsink &
Many developers have fixed this error by adding a startup delay or adjusting timeout parameters in gstnvarguscamerasrc.cpp.
2. Separate Terminals
If the above method does not work, run each camera stream in an independent terminal:
# Terminal 1
gst-launch-1.0 nvarguscamerasrc sensor_id=0 ! queue ! nv3dsink

# Terminal 2
gst-launch-1.0 nvarguscamerasrc sensor_id=1 ! queue ! nv3dsink
This method has been widely validated as stable in multi-camera scenarios.
3. Check sensor-id Configuration
On Jetson Orin NX platforms, the CAM0 interface sometimes requires sensor-id=1 instead of the default sensor-id=0. Please test:
gst-launch-1.0 nvarguscamerasrc sensor_id=1 ! nvvidconv ! nv3dsink
4. Check Frame Rate Settings
In DeepStream 6.2 / JetPack 5.1 environments, similar errors were often resolved by setting the frame rate to 30 FPS, while lower frame rates would trigger the failure. Although you are using JetPack 6.2, timing-related constraints may still apply, so we recommend verifying your frame rate configuration.
5. Reconfigure CSI Channels
Run the Jetson-IO tool to reconfigure CSI pins and make sure camera channels are properly enabled:
sudo /opt/nvidia/jetson-io/jetson-io.py
# Select Configure Jetson 24pin CSI Connector
# Select Configure for compatible hardware
# Select Camera IMX219 Dual
# Save changes and reboot
6. Confirm IMX219 Native Support
According to official compatibility documentation, IMX219 is natively supported in JetPack 6.2 (L4T R36.4.3) without extra driver installation. Your detection results have also confirmed this.
Related Technical Resources
For further troubleshooting, you may refer to these official and community resources:
Multi-camera solution for the same dmabuf_fd error:
NVIDIA Forums - nvarguscamerasrc + GMSL - dmabuf_fd
DeepStream 6.2 low-frame-rate related error:
NVIDIA Forums - Deepstream 6.2 sample app error
sensor-id configuration reference:
NVIDIA Forums - Capture image error
IMX219 / IMX477 compatibility on JetPack 6.2:
Seeed Studio Forum - IMX477 driver installing issue
Jetson-IO configuration guide:
NVIDIA Forums - DeepStream 6.2

We recommend trying Solution 1 (Startup Delay) and Solution 2 (Separate Terminals) first, as they are the most effective fixes reported by the developer community.

Thank you for your patience and support.

Issue Analysis
Based on your description:
✅ The IMX219 camera is correctly detected by the system (verified by nvargus_nvraw and I2C detection)
✅ The camera connects successfully ("Producer has connected")
❌ The streaming pipeline fails during the NVMM/DMA buffer handover stage
This means the driver and hardware communication are normal, while the problem lies in the resource allocation conflict inside the user-space multimedia framework (Argus/GStreamer).
Recommended Solutions
1. Single Terminal with Startup Delay
If you launch multiple camera streams in the same terminal or script, add a short delay between the two commands to avoid resource racing:
gst-launch-1.0 nvarguscamerasrc sensor_id=0 ! queue ! nv3dsink &
sleep 2 && gst-launch-1.0 nvarguscamerasrc sensor_id=1 ! queue ! nv3dsink &
Many developers have fixed this error by adding a startup delay or adjusting timeout parameters in gstnvarguscamerasrc.cpp.
2. Separate Terminals
If the above method does not work, run each camera stream in an independent terminal:
# Terminal 1
gst-launch-1.0 nvarguscamerasrc sensor_id=0 ! queue ! nv3dsink

# Terminal 2
gst-launch-1.0 nvarguscamerasrc sensor_id=1 ! queue ! nv3dsink
This method has been widely validated as stable in multi-camera scenarios.
3. Check sensor-id Configuration
On Jetson Orin NX platforms, the CAM0 interface sometimes requires sensor-id=1 instead of the default sensor-id=0. Please test:
gst-launch-1.0 nvarguscamerasrc sensor_id=1 ! nvvidconv ! nv3dsink
4. Check Frame Rate Settings
In DeepStream 6.2 / JetPack 5.1 environments, similar errors were often resolved by setting the frame rate to 30 FPS, while lower frame rates would trigger the failure. Although you are using JetPack 6.2, timing-related constraints may still apply, so we recommend verifying your frame rate configuration.
5. Reconfigure CSI Channels
Run the Jetson-IO tool to reconfigure CSI pins and make sure camera channels are properly enabled:
sudo /opt/nvidia/jetson-io/jetson-io.py
# Select Configure Jetson 24pin CSI Connector
# Select Configure for compatible hardware
# Select Camera IMX219 Dual
# Save changes and reboot
6. Confirm IMX219 Native Support
According to official compatibility documentation, IMX219 is natively supported in JetPack 6.2 (L4T R36.4.3) without extra driver installation. Your detection results have also confirmed this.
Related Technical Resources
For further troubleshooting, you may refer to these official and community resources:
Multi-camera solution for the same dmabuf_fd error:
NVIDIA Forums - nvarguscamerasrc + GMSL - dmabuf_fd
DeepStream 6.2 low-frame-rate related error:
NVIDIA Forums - Deepstream 6.2 sample app error
sensor-id configuration reference:
NVIDIA Forums - Capture image error
IMX219 / IMX477 compatibility on JetPack 6.2:
Seeed Studio Forum - IMX477 driver installing issue
Jetson-IO configuration guide:
NVIDIA Forums - DeepStream 6.2

We recommend trying Solution 1 (Startup Delay) and Solution 2 (Separate Terminals) first, as they are the most effective fixes reported by the developer community.

Thank you for your patience and support.

ticket_subject: Re: 回复：Re: 回复：Re: 回复：[## 342868 ##] reServer J501 : Technical Assistance - ROOTS
content:
Hi,

Yes, the BMCU (Board Management Control Unit) handles power management-related functions for the Jetson AGX Orin module. The BMCU is the EFM8SB10F8G microcontroller (U61 on Sheet 09)  that manages:

Power sequencing: Controls CARRIER_POWER_ON and MODULE_POWER_ON signals to the Jetson module
Power button debouncing: Handles the power button input and shutdown sequences
Reset control: Manages SYS_RST_IN_N and reset timing
Force shutdown: Provides emergency power-off functionality via EFM8_FORCE_SHUTDOWN
Power good monitoring: Monitors power rail status (PS_1V0_PG, PS_1V8_PG, etc.)

To determine the UART protocol and baud rate:
- Check Silicon Labs Documentation
EFM8SB10F8G Datasheet: Review UART0 module specifications (supports up to 460800 baud)
Refer to AN1095 (UART Communication) and AN1389 (Low Power UART)

Re: SKU#114993667 Product Defect Inquiry - Seeed Studio Response
Dear Cory,
Thank you for your timely feedback on the usage issue of SKU#114993667 from your end customer. We attach great importance to this situation and have reviewed the detailed fault description you provided regarding motor calibration, maximum position limits and computer pairing failures.
For the problems mentioned by the customer, we recommend that the end customer first follow the official standard operation tutorial to re-perform the relevant operations, as the tutorial contains detailed guidance on device connection, motor calibration and other key steps that can resolve most operation-related issues. The tutorial link is as follows:
https://wiki.seeedstudio.com/lerobot_soarm101_Rynnbot_Developer_Kit/
If the customer still encounters the same or other faults after operating in strict accordance with the above tutorial, could you please assist us in requesting the customer to provide clear photos of the operation steps up to the point where the fault occurs and a detailed description of the fault situation (including the specific operation step when the fault happens, any pop-up prompts, error messages, system status and other relevant details).
Once we receive the above information, our technical team will conduct a professional and accurate root cause analysis immediately, and feedback the corresponding solution to you as soon as possible.
Please feel free to contact us at any time if you have any other questions during the process. We appreciate your cooperation and support!

ticket_subject: 吕昭阳将客户问题转给技术支持团队核实，并要求未来的问题邮件抄送给他以便跟进。
content:
Hi,

1.Please confirm whether the host is a virtual machine, WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux), or a physical Ubuntu machine, and verify the Ubuntu version. The "USB timeout" error you’re encountering occurs because the device will restart during the flashing process:
For a physical Ubuntu machine, the device will reconnect automatically.
For a virtual machine, you need to manually click to "connect" the device.
For WSL, you need to separately "attach" the device.
We recommend using a physical Ubuntu machine for flashing to avoid such issues. Additionally:
When flashing JetPack 5.x, the recommended Ubuntu host versions are 18.04 and 20.04.
When flashing JetPack 6.x, the recommended Ubuntu host versions are 20.04 and 22.04.

2.Replace the USB cable with a high-quality one, and try using a different USB-A port on the host.

For a virtual machine, you need to manually click to "connect" the device.
For WSL, you need to separately "attach" the device.
We recommend using a physical Ubuntu machine for flashing to avoid such issues. Additionally:
When flashing JetPack 5.x, the recommended Ubuntu host versions are 18.04 and 20.04.
When flashing JetPack 6.x, the recommended Ubuntu host versions are 20.04 and 22.04.

2.Replace the USB cable with a high-quality one, and try using a different USB-A port on the host.

ticket_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#emily.masuda@gevernova.com;]
content:
Subject: Re: Technical Support for reComputer Industrial J4012 (SKU 110110191) – PoE Ethernet System Failure
Dear Ms. Masuda,
Thank you for contacting our technical support team regarding the complete system failure of your reComputer Industrial J4012 (SKU 110110191) after plugging in a PoE-enabled Ethernet cable. We take this industrial deployment issue seriously and will walk you through the root cause, risk analysis, and step-by-step recovery & prevention solutions below.
 Root Cause: PoE Port Design Misuse & Power Conflict
Your reComputer Industrial J4012 is a fanless industrial edge AI device powered by the NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX 16GB module, built for robust industrial environments. The "computer died" symptom is directly tied to two core design constraints:
Port Function Misunderstanding: The dual GbE RJ45 ports have non-interchangeable roles—one is a PoE power output (PSE) port, and the other is a data-only port.
Power Input Limitation: The device does NOT support receiving PoE power (PD mode); its only valid power input is the dedicated 12V/5A DC power port.
 Key PoE Port Design Differences
The two Ethernet ports are engineered for distinct purposes, as outlined below:
Port Type
Function
Power Capability
Safe Usage Scenario
PoE-PSE Port
Power Sourcing Equipment (PSE)
Outputs 802.3af-compliant PoE (max 15W)
Connects to PD devices (e.g., IP cameras) that require PoE power
Standard Ethernet Port
Data-only transmission
No PoE capability
Connects to regular network devices (switches, routers) for network access
Critical Warning: The J4012 is designed to supply PoE power to external devices, not to receive PoE power from a switch/injector. Any attempt to feed PoE into its Ethernet ports will cause irreversible hardware damage or immediate system shutdown.
 Risk Scenarios & Failure Mechanisms
Your "died after plugging in PoE" symptom aligns with the most severe misuse scenario:
Fatal Misuse: Feeding PoE into the Device (PD Mode Error)
Mechanism: Inserting a PoE-powered cable (from a switch/injector) into either Ethernet port causes reverse power injection, which instantly overloads the Ethernet controller chip or motherboard power management circuit.
Symptoms: Immediate power loss, no LED indicators, inability to boot (matches your description).
Risk: Permanent hardware damage is highly likely in this case.
High-Risk Conflict: PoE-PSE + DC Power Overload
If the PoE-PSE port is connected to a high-power PD device (e.g., >10W IP camera) while the device is powered via DC, power load imbalance can trigger system instability, sudden reboots, or crashes.
The PoE-PSE port has a 15W maximum output limit; exceeding this triggers overload protection, which may manifest as port failure or system lockup.
Stability-Impacting Scenarios
Non-standard PoE devices (not 802.3af-compliant) can cause voltage mismatches, leading to network drops or system freezes.
Dual static IP configurations on both ports may trigger kernel-level errors on reboot, resulting in boot failures.
 Risk Mitigation & Prevention
We’ve categorized risks and corresponding preventive measures to avoid recurrence:
Risk Level
Trigger Condition
Consequence
Prevention
Critical
Feeding PoE into any Ethernet port
Permanent hardware damage, no boot
Use only the dedicated DC port for power; never connect PoE-powered cables to the device
High
PoE-PSE port connected to >10W PD device + DC power
System crash, reboots
Limit PoE-PSE connected devices to ≤10W; avoid full load on both power paths
Medium
Non-802.3af PoE devices connected
Network instability, port failure
Only use 802.3af-compliant PD devices with the PoE-PSE port
Low
Dual static IPs configured
Boot failure
Use DHCP for both ports, or set a static IP on only one port
 Emergency Recovery & Troubleshooting
If your device has already failed, follow these steps immediately:
Full Power Disconnection: Unplug the DC power adapter and all Ethernet/PoE cables. Let the device discharge for at least 30 minutes to clear residual voltage.
Hardware Inspection: Check for burnt smells, discoloration on Ethernet ports, or loose DC power connectors—these indicate physical damage.
Recovery Attempt: Reconnect only the original 12V/5A DC power adapter (no network cables) and try to power on the device.
✅ If it boots: The damage is likely minimal; we can proceed to configure ports safely.
❌ If it fails to boot: Permanent hardware damage is probable, and we will initiate an RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) for repair/replacement.
Safe Usage Guidelines (For Future Deployments)
To prevent this issue from happening again:
Power Input Rule: Always use the dedicated 12V/5A DC power port—PoE is never a valid power source for the J4012.
Port Labeling: Physically label the two ports: "PoE-PSE (Output Only)" and "Data Only" to avoid confusion.
PoE-PSE Power Management: Ensure devices connected to the PoE-PSE port consume ≤10W (leaving 5W for safety margin).
Network Configuration: Use DHCP for both ports, or set a static IP on only one port to avoid kernel conflicts.
Next Steps
Please reply to this email with the results of your recovery attempt (step 3 above) and any photos of the device ports (if you notice physical damage). We will prioritize your case to either restore functionality or initiate RMA support for your industrial AI deployment.

ticket_subject: 回复：RE: 回复：RE: 回复：[## 345379 ##] RE: Issue with m.2 connector on Jetson
content:
Hi Caleb,

Thank you for the additional explanation.

Based on the photos and the description provided earlier, our hardware team reviewed the case and found that the M.2 connector pads appear to have been mechanically pulled from the PCB. This type of damage typically occurs when a strong external force is applied to the connector area, such as during removal of the SSD or when the connector is subjected to mechanical stress.

Under normal installation conditions, tightening the M.2 fixing screw should not cause the connector itself to detach from the PCB. Because the solder pads have been torn from the board, the connector unfortunately cannot be reliably repaired.

For this reason, we are unable to provide a replacement or warranty support for this case.

We understand this situation is frustrating and sincerely apologize that we cannot offer further assistance here. If you have any other questions about the system or future setup recommendations, we would still be happy to help.

ticket_subject: Difficulties integrating Arducam IMX477 (B0273) with A603
content:
Hi,

this issue is most likely a software application-level problem. This error typically occurs when the NVMM (NVIDIA Memory Management) buffer fails to allocate or map correctly. Common causes include:

Insufficient memory: Shared memory on the Jetson is occupied by other processes.

Abnormal nvargus-daemon service: The daemon is not running properly.

Permission issues: The user lacks access rights to the GPU/camera.

Buffer format mismatch: Problems with nvvidconv conversion.

Multiplexing conflict: The camera is being used by another application.

This is an application-level issue. We recommend prioritizing AI troubleshooting and diagnosis first. If it is confirmed to be a hardware or driver problem, you can then provide us with the relevant error logs. Thank you for your cooperation.

Hi,

this board supports the BL-M8723DU1 module. You can solder it yourself if you have good soldering skills; otherwise, we recommend using our light customization service.

ticket_subject: Hello, I would like to know if this industrial computer with Jetson Orin NX 16GB supports Super Mode. Does it meet the thermal requirements for this update? Thank you.
content:
Hi Dear，
If you are using an Orin NX 16GB/8GB module, do not enable MAXN SUPER mode. The cooling capacity of J4011/J4012 is insufficient to support it, and forcing this mode may result in permanent damage to the module.

ticket_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#mwellandt@tecnicum.com;]
content:
Dear Matthia,
Thank you for your message and for using the reComputer Industrial J4012.
Regarding the 16-pin I/O connector and the 2-pin power connector marked in your picture, we are sorry to let you know that these two accessories are not sold separately by us.
For your second demonstration setup, we recommend sourcing compatible connectors locally or through online distributors based on the specifications of the original parts.
The relevant items are:
1 × 16-Pin Terminal Block for DIO
1 × 2-Pin Terminal Block Power Connector
DC power adapter side: 5.5 × 2.1 mm barrel connector
Unfortunately, we are not able to provide a separate order link or supplier part number for these accessories at this time.
raw:
Dear Matthia,
Thank you for your message and for using the reComputer Industrial J4012.
Regarding the 16-pin I/O connector and the 2-pin power connector marked in your picture, we are sorry to let you know that these two accessories are not sold separately by us.
For your second demonstration setup, we recommend sourcing compatible connectors locally or through online distributors based on the specifications of the original parts.
The relevant items are:
1 × 16-Pin Terminal Block for DIO
1 × 2-Pin Terminal Block Power Connector
DC power adapter side: 5.5 × 2.1 mm barrel connector
Unfortunately, we are not able to provide a separate order link or supplier part number for these accessories at this time.

ticket_subject: Return request for return authorization
content:
Hi,

Sorry, we can only refund devices that remain sealed. 

At the moment, we do not have a confirmed permanent fix yet. As a workaround, we recommend using the onboard Ethernet port instead of USB LAN for SSH and remote access, especially when the host is running Windows. This has been more stable in our testing. We are continuing to investigate the root cause of the intermittent USB serial and SSH disconnections.

We are continuing to investigate the root cause of the intermittent USB serial and SSH disconnections.

ticket_subject: I am using the A203. Lately i updated to the 32.7.6 with a TX2 NX. Curently i am trying to use the UART on the 40pin header, but it seems to be linked to the device console.
content:
Dear ,
Thank you for your message.
From your description, it appears that the UART on the 40-pin header is currently configured as the system console, which is why it is not behaving as a normal user UART.
As a first step, please check whether the serial console service is enabled on your system. In many cases, this UART may be occupied by the console service by default. You may also want to check whether the boot configuration includes console settings assigned to that UART.
If possible, please share the following information with us so we can confirm further:
the exact UART device node you are using
whether you see boot logs printed on that port
the output of the relevant serial console service status and boot configuration
Once we have that information, we can help verify how to free this UART for normal use on the A203 with TX2 NX and L4T 32.7.6.

ticket_subject: reComputer Robotics J3011 not displaying video
content:
Hi Dear,
Thank you for your update, and I’m glad to hear that reflashing resolved the boot issue.
Regarding the new behavior you observed, this may not be a full boot failure. Based on your description, it is more likely related to the display initialization or the USB-C to DisplayPort handshake during power-on.
In other words, the system may still be booting normally, but the video output is not established correctly when the USB-C to DisplayPort cable is already connected before startup.
To help us narrow this down, could you please provide the following information:
JetPack version
Jetson module model
Carrier board model
Monitor model
USB-C to DisplayPort cable or adapter model
Whether the device is reachable via SSH when this issue occurs
If possible, a serial boot log captured when powering on with the cable connected
In the meantime, we also suggest trying the following quick checks:
Test with a different USB-C to DisplayPort cable
Test with another monitor if available
Check whether the issue occurs with a different adapter or display combination
These steps will help us determine whether this is related to cable/monitor compatibility or to the display initialization sequence on boot.
Once we have the above information, we will be happy to investigate further with you.

ticket_subject: Xaiver AGX H01 kit flashing not available
content:
Hi,

1. The Jetson AGX Xavier module itself is supported until approximately 2028 (per NVIDIA’s product lifecycle).
2. You mentioned the device screen is completely unresponsive. Could you please provide a complete video of your operation process so we can analyze the issue?
3. We recommend troubleshooting in the following order:

0. Make sure you are using a physical Ubuntu host machine, not a virtual machine. If using a VM, confirm the device is properly passed through to the VM.
1. Try replacing the USB‑C cable.
2. Confirm you are connected to the **OTG USB port**.
3. Follow the correct Recovery mode sequence.
4. Remove the NVMe SSD.
5. Switch to an Ubuntu 18.04 host machine.
6. Run `lsusb` to check if the device is detected.

Do you want me to make this even more concise for a quick support reply?

1. Try replacing the USB‑C cable.
2. Confirm you are connected to the **OTG USB port**.
3. Follow the correct Recovery mode sequence.
4. Remove the NVMe SSD.
5. Switch to an Ubuntu 18.04 host machine.
6. Run `lsusb` to check if the device is detected.

Hi,
  I noticed you mentioned it was not a quality issue but we noticed when putting a drive in the tolerance is very tight between the board and the m.2 port. We’ve been having some issues with overheating in our ssds lately so we put one on with a heatsync and I believe the tolerance isn’t big enough to allow this even though it’s a standard drive still.
  For reference this is the drive used:
Crucial P310 1TB PCIe Gen4 NVMe 2280 M.2 SSD with heatsink | CT1000P310SSD5 | crucial.com
Is there any limitations listed that this is a known issue? We had checked to make sure beforehand and there didn’t seem to be any.
  Kind regards,

ticket_subject: Technical Inquiry: Enabling fTPM on reComputer Jetson Industrial J4012 (Orin NX 16GB)
content:
Dear Shumpei,
Thank you for contacting Seeed Studio.
Regarding your inquiry about enabling TPM functionality on the reComputer Industrial J4012, we would like to provide the following information.
According to our internal testing and documentation, the TPM interface on the reComputer Industrial series has been verified to function correctly when used with an external TPM module (dTPM). You may refer to the following documentation for details:
https://wiki.seeedstudio.com/reComputer_Industrial_J20_Hardware_Interfaces_Usage/#tpm
This interface allows users to connect a discrete TPM module via the hardware interface, which has been validated on our platform.
Regarding fTPM (Firmware TPM), this feature is implemented within the Jetson SoC security architecture and OP-TEE environment provided by NVIDIA. The enablement process, including fuse configuration and TOS/OP-TEE setup, follows the NVIDIA Jetson security framework described in the official NVIDIA documentation.
Since fTPM is managed by the Jetson module itself, no additional hardware modification or carrier-board-specific patch is required from the Seeed side when following NVIDIA’s official guide.
However, please note that burning security fuses is an irreversible operation, and incorrect configuration may permanently lock the device. We therefore recommend carefully following NVIDIA’s official documentation and validating the procedure in a controlled environment before deployment.
If you have further questions during your implementation, please feel free to contact us.

This interface allows users to connect a discrete TPM module via the hardware interface, which has been validated on our platform.
Regarding fTPM (Firmware TPM), this feature is implemented within the Jetson SoC security architecture and OP-TEE environment provided by NVIDIA. Since fTPM is managed by the Jetson module itself, no additional hardware modification or carrier-board-specific patch is required from the Seeed side when following NVIDIA’s official guide.
However, please note that burning security fuses is an irreversible operation, and incorrect configuration may permanently lock the device. We therefore recommend carefully following NVIDIA’s official documentation and validating the procedure in a controlled environment before deployment.
If you have further questions during your implementation, please feel free to contact us.

ticket_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#sidney.nimako@scale.com;]
content:
Hi Dear,
Thank you for your question.
Unfortunately, the reComputer Robotics J5012 with GMSL extension board is not directly compatible with ZED X / ZED X Mini cameras.
The main reason is that integration with ZED X / ZED X Mini requires the corresponding driver support and related files from Stereolabs, such as the camera driver, device tree support, and other required software components for the GMSL camera pipeline. At this time, we do not have these files from ZED X / ZED X Mini, so we are unable to adapt or validate this camera on our side.
For this reason, we are not able to provide an adaptation service or guarantee interoperability with ZED X / ZED X Mini on this product.
We recommend contacting Stereolabs directly to check whether they can provide the required driver package and support for this hardware combination.
raw:
Hi Dear,
Thank you for your question.
Unfortunately, the reComputer Robotics J5012 with GMSL extension board is not directly compatible with ZED X / ZED X Mini cameras.
The main reason is that integration with ZED X / ZED X Mini requires the corresponding driver support and related files from Stereolabs, such as the camera driver, device tree support, and other required software components for the GMSL camera pipeline. At this time, we do not have these files from ZED X / ZED X Mini, so we are unable to adapt or validate this camera on our side.
For this reason, we are not able to provide an adaptation service or guarantee interoperability with ZED X / ZED X Mini on this product.
We recommend contacting Stereolabs directly to check whether they can provide the required driver package and support for this hardware combination.

ticket_subject: 回复：回复：Re: [EXT] 回复：[## 342600 ##] Request for Lifecycle / EOL Status – 102991695
content:
Hi Nimit,
Thank you for your careful review. I understand your questions regarding the differences in lead content reported in the two documents.
The discrepancy primarily stems from the different testing methodologies employed in the 2022 assessment:
1. Different Testing Approaches
●
RoHS Test: Applied single-point testing, where each component was tested individually. This method accurately identified that components A-93, A-148, and A-152 contain lead.
●
REACH Test: Utilized a group testing approach (refer to “Component Description” on page 5 of the report). The sample was categorized into three groups:
○
A001: Non-metal group
○
A002: Metal group
○
A003: Electronic component group
2. Limitations of Group Testing
●
The REACH report only presents the overall test result for the A002 metal group and does not include data for individual components within that group.
●
In reality, the A002 group comprises multiple metal parts (as listed on pages 7–9 of the report, parts A-2 through A-154).
●
Consequently, the lead content from certain parts may have been diluted by other parts in the group, making it impossible to accurately identify specific components containing lead.
3. Key Conclusion
Due to the differing methodologies, the lead content data from the two reports are not directly comparable.
4. Background Context
●
This REACH report reflects testing conducted in 2022, when group testing was still a relatively common screening method.
●
However, per current ECHA requirements, assessments must now be based on each homogeneous material or individual component. The group testing approach is no longer acceptable.
●
If each metal component were to be re-tested using single-point testing under current REACH standards, the results would comply—as confirmed by RoHS single-point testing, which shows that the lead-containing components fall under the copper alloy exemption.
Please feel free to reach out if you have further questions.
Best regards,
Zheng Xu
Seeed Technology Co., Ltd.

ticket_subject: 用户询问关于Jetson通过GPIO从休眠状态唤醒的测试进展，并表示该问题已成为高优先级，同时请求提供可测试的建议。
content:
Hi Giulio,
Apologies for the delayed follow-up, and thank you for your patience.
After confirming internally with our R&D team, we would like to inform you that the reComputer Industrial platform does not support hardware-level ultra-low-power wake-up from hibernation.
This means that wake-up mechanisms such as Wake-on-LAN or GPIO wake from a deep hibernation/ultra-low-power state are currently not supported on this platform at the hardware level.
We understand that this feature is important for your application, and we sincerely apologize for any inconvenience this may cause.
If you would like, you can share more details about your use case, and we would be happy to explore possible alternative approaches or configurations that may help meet your requirements.

ticket_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#henrique.palos@ouronova.com;]
content:
Hi,

Thank you for reaching out. To help diagnose the no-video issue on your reComputer Robotics J3011, please assist us by performing the following troubleshooting steps:

Step 1: Verify Power Supply
Although the LED is on (indicating basic power), please confirm the actual power delivery is sufficient:
- If using a DC adapter: Ensure the output is ≥19V/1.32A (25W) or higher current rating
- The XT30 interface supports 19-54V, but insufficient current (even with correct voltage) can prevent the system from fully booting the display output

Step 2: Verify Cable and Display
- Use a passive USB-C to DP cable (not active)
- Try flipping the USB-C connector 180 degrees
- Ensure the monitor is switched to the correct DP input source

Step 3: Recovery Mode Hardware Check (Critical)
1. Short the REC and GND pins on the carrier board (or hold the REC button)
2. Connect the USB-C to your host PC
3. On the host, run `lsusb` and check for NVIDIA Corp device (VID:PID such as 0955:7323)
   - If detected: Hardware is functioning; the issue is likely corrupted pre-installed system. Re-flashing is required
   - If not detected: Potential hardware defect; please contact us for RMA

Please let us know the results of these checks, particularly Step 3.

ticket_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#marco.stroppiana@edu.unito.it;]
content:
Hi Dear,
Thank you for the detailed description of the issue.
Based on your report, the Ethernet interface is functioning at the local network level (DHCP assigned correctly, gateway reachable, and default route present), but outbound internet traffic is not succeeding. Since the same cable and switch port work with another device, this suggests the issue is likely related to routing, network policy, or configuration rather than a physical hardware fault.
To further isolate the cause, please help us collect the following information:
Confirm the default route
Please run:
ip route
Ensure the output shows:
default via 192.168.10.1 dev eth0
Test the routing path
Please run:
traceroute 8.8.8.8
(or tracepath 8.8.8.8 if traceroute is unavailable)
This will help determine whether traffic stops at the gateway.
Check policy routing rules
Please run:
ip rule
ip route show table all
Disable Ethernet offloading features (for testing)
Some Jetson systems may experience abnormal behavior related to hardware offload. Please run:
sudo ethtool -K eth0 tso off gso off gro off
Then test again:
ping 8.8.8.8
Provide JetPack/L4T version
Please run:
cat /etc/nv_tegra_release
Additionally, please let us know:
Are other Jetson devices able to access the internet on the same network?
Since the device can reach the gateway successfully, this usually indicates that the Ethernet hardware is functioning correctly. In most cases, similar symptoms are related to gateway NAT configuration, network policy restrictions, or routing behavior.
Once we receive the requested outputs, we will analyze them and provide the next steps promptly.

ticket_subject: Fw:Re:Request for Guidance on Customer Query on this part 114110207- Jetson产品线
content:
Hi ,
Based on your description, especially the fact that:
The device automatically enters Force Recovery Mode without pressing the recovery button
It is detected as 0955:7023 (NVIDIA APX) via USB
The board cannot exit recovery state during SDK Manager flashing
No normal boot LED sequence or display output is observed
This behavior suggests that the recovery-related hardware circuit (Force Recovery signal path) may be abnormal. It is possible that the recovery pin is being continuously asserted at hardware level, which would prevent the system from booting normally.
Since standard flashing and power cycle procedures have already been verified, this case is likely a hardware-level issue.
To proceed with further evaluation and possible repair/replacement (RMA process), kindly provide the following information: If the device is still under warranty, please return it to Seeed for inspection and repair.
To proceed, please fill out a ticket at the following link: https://aftersale.seeedstudio.com/

Aftersale Type: Repair
Is return required: Yes
* Note: When packing your device, include all accessories. 

Please check the images and the .pdf instructions on how to create such a ticket. After you've done that, please share us the after-sales ticket number: (A-0000-XXXX)

1. The button to create the ticket is located on the bottom of the page

2. After creating, you can find the number at 'My Tickets'

We appreciate your patience and understanding. Feel free to ask me if you have more questions.

However, if your order was not placed directly with us but through another distributor, we regret that we cannot process the after-sale service using their order number. Please contact the distributor directly, inform them that your issue has been verified by us, and request after-sales service from them. They will handle your request according to their own policies.

ticket_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#jmartinez@cetim.es;]
content:
Dear jmartinez,
Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention and for sharing the information from NVIDIA’s Serial Number Lookup Tool.
We understand your concern regarding the Jetson Orin NX Hardware Errata-2 (“Logic Component Out of Specification”) and the potential impact on your reComputer Super J4012 system.
According to NVIDIA’s published errata, certain Jetson Orin NX modules manufactured between October 2024 and May 2025 may experience improper shutdown behavior when operating above 12V input, which in some cases could lead to data corruption. NVIDIA’s interim guidance recommends limiting module operation to a 5V–12V input range.
As you correctly noted, the reComputer Super J4012 system is designed to operate with a higher system input voltage. Internally, the carrier board regulates and distributes power to the module; however, if the installed Orin NX module belongs to the affected production batch identified by NVIDIA, it may still exhibit the behavior described in the errata.
To properly assess your case and determine the appropriate resolution, we kindly ask you to provide the following information:
Your original order number (or distributor purchase reference)
The product serial number of your reComputer Super J4012
The serial number of the Jetson Orin NX module (if accessible)
A brief description of the observed SSD corruption behavior (e.g., frequency, shutdown conditions, error logs if available)
This information will allow us to verify the procurement batch of the installed module and confirm whether it falls within the NVIDIA-identified affected range. Once confirmed, we will coordinate internally and with NVIDIA as needed to determine the appropriate corrective action, which may include module replacement or RMA processing in accordance with the official policy.
We appreciate your cooperation and patience while we investigate this matter. Please share the requested details at your earliest convenience so we can proceed.

We hope you have a lot of fun playing with your new robotic hand.
Please stay tuned to our updates, as we are working on more exciting new products that you might also enjoy.

ticket_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#hyungi5754@kookmin.ac.kr;]
content:
Hello,
Thanks for your question.
The Robotics J401 carrier board specifies a power input range of 19–54V via the XT30 (2+2) connector. A “48V” lithium battery pack typically reaches ~54.6V when fully charged (e.g., a 13S Li-ion/LiPo pack). Since 54.6V exceeds the stated 54V maximum, we do not recommend connecting the battery directly at full charge.
Even a small over-voltage margin can become a reliability risk, and in real robotics systems you can also see additional transient spikes during load changes, cable inductance effects, motor start/stop, etc.
raw:
Hello,
Thanks for your question.
The Robotics J401 carrier board specifies a power input range of 19–54V via the XT30 (2+2) connector. A “48V” lithium battery pack typically reaches ~54.6V when fully charged (e.g., a 13S Li-ion/LiPo pack). Since 54.6V exceeds the stated 54V maximum, we do not recommend connecting the battery directly at full charge.
Even a small over-voltage margin can become a reliability risk, and in real robotics systems you can also see additional transient spikes during load changes, cable inductance effects, motor start/stop, etc.

ticket_subject: Re: Your receipt from Pollen Robotics SAS #2239-3944
content:
Dear Blaise,
Thank you for providing the photo.
Upon reviewing the image with our technical team, we would like to clarify the design of the Reachy Mini. The camera is actually designed to be placed in the center, directly between the two eyes, and the eyes themselves do not contain cameras or individual lenses.
In the photo you shared, we can clearly see the camera flex cable, which indicates that the camera module is present and you are not missing the lens component.
Please proceed with the assembly using the central camera module. If you need any further assistance or have questions during the setup, please feel free to let us know!

content:
RM
Rmclrobotics
.
2月 24 12:17 下午
( 45天前 )
Hi Dayu,

        I think the DI/DO can only be used to emulate handshaking once the J501 is powered on, but here we have only one dedicated DC-DC supply with the below mentioned 5VSB/PS_ON pins that is responsible for powering up the J501.

The J501 will not be powered on until PS_ON is pulled to low, which cannot be emulated using DI/DO like you suggested if the J501 is itself not booted yet.

Kindly let us know your suggestions for industrial power sequence, and also share us details regarding "J17" 20 pin header.

Thanks & Regards,

JK Roopan

(0422-4330285)

Coimbatore - 641 006. India.

ticket_subject: RE: Technical Support Email (UK) - 22207853
content:
Hi,

thank you for your message. Regarding your question, our suggestions are as follows:
On the reComputer J401 carrier board:

- For Feetech TTL motors: Use the onboard UART (3.3V). Since the motor TX is 5V, a 5V→3.3V level shifter is required.
- For Robstride CAN motors: Use the onboard CAN interface directly; no USB-CAN adapter is needed.

Do not use GPIO for simulated communication, as its stability and speed are inferior to hardware UART/CAN.
The two interfaces can be used at the same time without conflict.

The two interfaces can be used at the same time without conflict.
